title: Aloo Matar Bhaji

ingredients.title: Primary
items: 3 medium potatoes (about 400g) – peeled, diced ½ inch cubes
2 tbsp oil (mustard oil or vegetable oil)

ingredients.title: Ground Spices
items: ½ tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)

ingredients.title: Finishing
items: 1 tbsp fresh cilantro (coriander leaves) – finely chopped

instruction.title: Method
steps: Prep the Ingredients: Dice the potatoes evenly for even cooking. If using frozen peas, soak in warm water for 5–10 minutes and drain. Measure and keep all spices ready (mise en place).

Tempering & Aromatics: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat. Add cumin seeds (and mustard seeds if using). Let them crackle. Add slit green chili and chopped onions. Sauté until golden (5–6 mins). Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté until raw smell disappears (1 min).
Spice Base & Tomato: Add chopped tomatoes. Cook until soft and oil begins to separate. Stir in turmeric, coriander powder, red chili powder. Mix well and cook for 1–2 minutes to bloom the spices.

Cook Potatoes & Peas: Add diced potatoes, stir to coat with masala. Add green peas, salt, and about 2–3 tbsp water. Mix. Cover and cook on low-medium heat for 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender. (Add a splash of water only if needed to avoid burning.)
Final Seasoning: Once potatoes are cooked, sprinkle garam masala. Stir gently to mix everything. Cook uncovered for another 2–3 minutes for slight crisping at the base.
Finish & Serve: Turn off heat. Add lemon juice (optional) and fresh coriander. Serve hot with phulka, chapati, poori, or rice.

chefTips: Oil: Mustard oil adds authenticity; heat it to smoking point before using.
Flavor boost: Add a pinch of amchur (dry mango powder) or squeeze of lemon juice for tanginess.
Variation: Add kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) at the end for depth.
Texture: Avoid over-stirring after potatoes are soft to prevent them from breaking down.
